notes: Use the stabilized home structure and CSS; do not inherit any previous scenario semantics from example data.
Only the second-screen content area is intended to vary freely. Header banner, query card, and bottom tabbar should stay template-first.

For conversion, price benefit, points, or pre-booking awareness tasks, place the core message in the query card or adjacent decision slot first; add second-screen content only when it has independent user value.

When adding a new benefit to the query card, preserve the baseline search labels and controls; use additive fields such as search.benefitTag instead of replacing stateLabel, toggleLabel, trip type, or check labels.

The fifth-row search check labels in the header/query area stay locked as '学生票' and '高铁动车'; layout keeps the second option right-aligned to the destination column, and checkbox icons default to the unchecked asset unless checked is explicitly true.
